newsSince: B. Riley raised its ROG price target to $200 from $165 (Buy) on June 15, helping drive a +16% move since June 9 and a new 52-week high on June 22. Q1 design wins in automotive/radar (Asian OEM) ramp into H2 2026, while data-center materials remain mostly sampling/prototype with meaningful revenue not expected until late 2027. No M&A, guidance change, or fresh design win since June 9 beyond the sell-side upgrade.

sources.label: B. Riley PT raise / 52-week high (Markets Daily)

verdicts.rankRationale: A June 15 sell-side PT hike to $200 plus a new 52-week high (now +78% YTD) confirms the name is no longer hidden/under-owned, eroding the residual-alpha premise this page rewards. No new fundamental win; data-center revenue still ~2027.
verdicts.bull: AI-adjacent thermal/laminate supplier with auto/radar design wins ramping H2 2026 and rising sell-side targets.
verdicts.neutral: Strong Q1 and an upgrade, but data-center revenue is years out and the stock has already re-rated hard.
verdicts.bear: Now widely flagged after a 52-week high; the under-owned mispricing this page prizes is gone.

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent AI-node lens rewards the thermal/high-frequency/power-electronics link, which the B. Riley upgrade and ramping design wins modestly reinforce. Data-center exposure validated but distant (late 2027), so a small positive, not a rank move at #7.
verdicts.bull: Thermal-interface and high-frequency materials feed AI accelerators and power electronics; design wins ramping and targets rising.
verdicts.neutral: Real AI-supply-chain link, but the data-center contribution is still sampling/prototype until 2027.
verdicts.bear: Cyclical electronics demand and activist-sensitive sentiment after a large run.
